calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-27-2023 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-17-2023 Continued To Jul-25-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Plaintiffs Motion For Default Judgment Pursuant To Ccp 558(D)
Off Calendar May-10-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for motion for default judgment.

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 11:27:12 a.m. and concluding at 11:56:42 a.m.

Plaintiff John Sykes appearing represented by Joseph Stephen Tobener, Esq. and Rebecca Didlake, Esq.
Defendant Larkin Derek Ray not appearing.

The following witnesses are sworn to testify on behalf of Plaintiff:

1. JOHN SYKES
2. DAVID CHESNOSKY
3. CLAUDIO BLUER

Counsel for Plaintiff presents summary of case and an offer of proof.
Counsel for Plaintiff presents offer of proof for the testimony of DAVID CHESNOSKY and CLAUDIO BLUER. Court shall proceed on the Declaration of Claudio Bluer in Support of Application for Default Judgment filed Mar-13-2023 and the Declaration of David Chesnosky in Support of Application for Default Judgment filed Apr-27-2023. Witnesses are excused.

JOHN SYKES is examined by the Court. Witness attests that its Declaration filed on Mar-13-2023 and comments made by its counsel are true and correct.

Court determines the following:

1. Default against Defendant Larkin Derek Ray was entered on Sep-14-2022
2. DOES 1-10 were DISMISSED Apr-25-2023

The matter is submitted.
Based on the First Amended Complaint, supporting evidence presented, Declarations of Claudio Bluer and David Chesnosky, Plaintiff's Declaration, the Court finds the essential elements of the First Amended Complaint have been proven. Defendant owed a duty of habitability of the premises and it breached that duty.

Plaintiff's requests are ruled upon as follows:

A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H, I, J, K, L, M, N, O, and R are GRANTED.
P, Q, and S are WITHDRAWN.

After consideration and good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff John Sykes, and against Defendant Larkin Derek Ray, as follows:

1. Damages: $1,349,300.80

TOTAL JUDGMENT: $1,349,300.80
Proposed judgment to be submitted.
